data.title: Bitcoin retreats from $100K, bringing down other crypto, related stocks
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) retrenched some on Thursday afternoon after holding above the $100K mark for less than 24 hours.The token rose as high as $104K, a rally of almost 50% since Trump's presidential victory, that was given a last push by Trump's pick of pro-crypto Paul Atkins to head the Securities and Exchange Commission.

At 3:15 PM ET, the oldest of digital tokens traded at ~$98,5K, according to CryptoCompare. Many other crypto assets also pulled back. Ethereum (ETH-USD) slipped 0.5% to $3.83K, solana (SOL-USD) dipped 0.3%, cardano (ADA-USD) - 0.5%, and XRP (XRP-USD) -2.2%.

Bitcoin-linked stocks followed suit. MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) slid 5.1%, Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) -2.5%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) dipped 3.7%, MARA Holdings (MARA) -2.5%, Bit Digital (BTBT) -7.1%.

data.title: HSBC's private-banking CEO leaving at month's end
TotalEnergies upgraded at RBC with 'quality on sale' after stock price lags peers
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) +1.4% in Thursday's trading as RBC Capital upgrades to Outperform from Sector Perform with an €80 price target, seeing the stock's recent selloff as providing an opportunity for long term investors to build a position ahead of material growth in the coming years.

TotalEnergies (TTE) has materially underperformed the sector recently, which RBC's Biraj Borkhataria attributes to uncertainty in French politics as well as company specific issues related to investments in Adani, which has raised questions around the company's due diligence.

The company also has been weighed by some soft results and persistent downgrades to earnings potential for liquefied natural gas, the analyst says.

Borkataria notes TotalEnergies (TTE) does not have material exposure to French corporate tax given most of its profits are derived from outside the country, while a buyback tax also likely would be immaterial in the context of the wider group.

While the risk premium around French politics could linger for some time, but Borkhataria believes the actual monetary risk is more than reflected in TotalEnergies' (TTE) lagging performance compared to peers.

data.title: Lilly's Zepbound will first be given in the UK to those with 'highest clinical need'
data.description:
Given the strong demand expected for Eli Lilly's (NYSE:LLY) weight loss treatment Zepbound (tirzepatide) in the UK, the country's National Institute for Health and Care Excellence ("NICE") said the drug will first be offered to people with the "highest clinical need."

Zepbound has been recommended by NICE for those with a body mass index of greater than 35 and at least one illness related to being overweight. The organization, responsible for ensuring evidence-based best practices and value for health care, said that about 3.4M people in the country meet these criteria.

"The roll out of tirzepatide has to be carefully managed to ensure healthcare professionals can continue to meet the full range of health needs of all their patients," NICE said.

Final guidance is expected on Dec. 23, barring any appeals. Assuming there aren't, the first patients can receive Zepbound after 90 days if they are already receiving weight management services, or 180 days if they have the highest clinical priority.

NICE added that about 220,000 people are expected to get Zepbound during the three-year rollout period. "During this time evidence will be generated to understand the most effective forms of care, and this will inform a NICE re-evaluation of the remaining roll out period to the wider group of eligible patients."

data.title: Bantec's Howco Distributing and Bantec Environmental win contracts
data.description:
• Bantec (OTCPK:BANT) has won contracts through its subsidiaries Howco Distributing and Bantec Environmental.
  • "Howco Distributing had sales of 1.163M for the past three months with one sizable contract of $0.46M." said Michael Bannon, Bantec's Chairman and CEO.
  • Bantec Environmental won its first fire cleanup contract; Bantec Environmental is now a licensed asbestos removal contractor in Connecticut.

data.title: Abercrombie & Fitch down on $3M shares of insider trading
data.description:
• Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF) fell about 8% on Thursday after the company disclosed that insiders sold about $3M shares.
  • Suzanne M. Coulter, a director at ANF, sold 6,800 shares of the company's Class A common stock at a price of $161.802 per share, for a total transaction value of approximately $1.1M.
  • Executive VP, Gregory Henchel, sold about $1.5M worth of the company’s stock at $150 per share.
  • Kenneth Robinson, director of the company, also sold about 1,100 class A common stock for a price of $161.74 per share. Robinson now holds 7,272 class A stock of the company.

data.title: ConocoPhillips upgraded, CNX cut at J.P. Morgan; says secular tailwinds favor gas over oil
data.description:
ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) +1.3% in Thursday's trading as J.P. Morgan upgrades to Overweight from Neutral with a $123 price target, largely reflecting the stock's YTD underperformance vs. large-cap energy exploration and production companies.

ConocoPhillips (COP) recently raised its synergy capture estimate from the Marathon Oil acquisition to $1B, and JPM analysts led by Arun Jayaram believes the company will be one of the few E&Ps in their coverage that raise cash returns in 2025, including $6B of buybacks that should provide support for the stock.

At the same time, JPM downgrades CNX Resources (CNX) to Underweight from Neutral with a $37 PT, saying valuation seems stretched compared to gas-oriented peers.

Additionally, the analysts say they are positive on natural gas fundamentals, and CNX (CNX) has less near-term gas exposure vs. gassy peers given its robust hedge book.

Devon Energy (DVN) and SM Energy (SM) are both cut to Neutral from Overweight, with respective $43 and $53 PTs; a factor supporting the Devon downgrade is the sizable P/E overhang following the Grayson Mill deal.

J.P. Morgan says its top natural gas picks remain Overweight-rated Antero Resources (AR), EQT (EQT) and Gulfport Energy (GPOR), while its favorites for oil exposure are ConocoPhillips (COP), Coterra Energy (CTRA) and Permian Resources (PR).

data.title: Equity REITs raise record capital in Q3 through ATM offerings - report
data.description:
Equity REITs raised $7.21B through at-the-market offering programs in Q3 2024, an all-time quarterly high, a report by S&P Global Market Intelligence showed.

Comparatively, the sector raised $3.22B in the prior quarter. The Q3 2024 activity increased 41.1% on a yearly basis, the analysis said.

Health care REIT Welltower (WELL) is said to have raised the most proceeds through ATM issuances, with the gross proceeds coming at $1.25B.

Datacenter REIT Equinix (EQIX) sold 569,382 shares to raise $467M in net proceeds. EQIX also settled its remaining 643,428 shares of outstanding forward-sale agreements for $509M, the report noted.

Among subsectors, healthcare REITs reportedly raised the most capital through ATM programs. The subsector raised $2.65B in capital.

Datacenter was the next on the list, with $1.78B raised during the quarter.

data.title: OpenAI pushes monetization efforts further with new $200/month plan
data.description:
• Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) OpenAI is testing the limits of monetizing its artificial intelligence models as it announced a $200 per month subscription plan for its new ChatGPT Pro option.
  • The Sam Altman-led startup revealed the new plan during a livestream today in what is Day 1 of its "12 Days of OpenAI."
  • "Today we're also adding ChatGPT Pro, a new plan that allows us to offer the best of our models & tools at scale, including unlimited access to OpenAI o1 and a Pro-only version of o1 that thinks longer for even more reliable responses," OpenAI posted on X.
  • ChatGPT Pro offers unlimited access to ChatGPT 4o and o1, its newest reasoning model that is capable of completing more complex tasks. o1 is also capable of completing advanced math and coding problems.
  • The Pro plan is available starting today and is quite a jump in price from its Plus plan, which costs $20 per month.

data.title: Equity REITs continue to reduce NAV discount
data.description:
Equity real estate investment trusts in the U.S. that are publicly listed ended Nov. 29 at a median 6.5% discount as compared to their consensus net asset value per share estimates, according to S&P Global Market Intelligence data.

The discount was 2.8 percentage points below the median discount of 9.3% as of Nov. 1.

The hotel sector continued to be the largest median discount to net asset value (NAV) at 21.6%, down compared to a median discount of 27.0% in the prior month.

The other sectors that preceeded were timber at 18.6%, farmland at 17.1% and office at 14.6%.

Datacenter REITs traded at the largest median premium to NAV, at 27.6%, followed by the healthcare sector.

Industrial Logistics Properties Trust (ILPT) traded at the largest discount to NAV among all U.S. REITs with at least $200 million in market capitalization, according to the report. Hudson Pacific Properties (NYSE:HPP) took the second spot.

Seven of the 10 public REITs with a market capitalization of at least $200 million that traded at the largest premiums, were from the healthcare sector, including Welltower (NYSE:WELL)
